_comment: compile.py configuration for the God Hand matching-decomp build.
Paths are relative to the repo root.
_as_flags_doc: padding for `.text`/`.data`/etc.  Without it, a carved 4-byte
function .o ends up with a 16-byte .text (1 nop + 12 zero-padding
bytes), which the linker concatenates into the output .text and
breaks retail byte-equality.  The monolithic asm/cod/000000.s
unit's .text happens to be a multiple of 16 (2,867,072 bytes), so
the flag is a no-op for the empty-carves baseline (verified)
but mandatory the moment any function is carved.
_section_flag_overrides_doc: Splat emits .section .text, "ax" and .section .data, "wa".
Retail PS2 ELF has SHF_WRITE set on both .text and .data
(likely for overlay self-patching). objcopy --set-section-flags is
applied to each .o after assembly to add the missing W bit so ld
inherits the right flags into the final output sections.
.text gets the W bit forced on via 'code' (SHF_EXECINSTR) + 'data'
(SHF_WRITE in binutils's internal flag set); ld then inherits WAX
into the linked .text.
.data is intentionally NOT given the X bit here, even though retail
ELF has .data as WAX (likely for runtime overlay self-patching).
Two reasons: (1) the input .o's .data is genuinely data, not code,
so giving it the X bit poisons objdiff's section classification:
objdiff infers section kind from flags (X -> code) and would count
those bytes under total_code instead of total_data. (2) The X bit
on the OUTPUT .data
is stamped back on after the link by scripts/elf_postprocess.py's
LOADED_SECTION_PATCHES table, which forces retail sh_flags=0x07
(WAX) onto .data unconditionally. So objdiff sees .data as WA in
every .o (correctly classified as data), and the linked ELF still
comes out byte-identical to retail with .data sh_flags=WAX.

sdata_overrides._comment: Per-source -G<n> overrides; empty until decompilation starts.

_compile_units_doc: Per-TU C compiler override.  Each entry is
where `compiler` is one of 'cygnus-2.96' (default, today's behaviour
for all 735 currently-matched TUs) or 'sn-2.95.3-136' (opt-in SN
ee-gcc for the `sq`-prologue family).  TUs not
named here use the default compiler; the override is at TU
granularity (not per-function — that's a later task if needed).
Documentation entries (path starting with `_`) are ignored.
or SN cc1.exe (wibo).  cpp0 and ee-as stay shared between paths so
the ELF flags (0x20924001, eabi64) match retail regardless of which
Example shape (do not uncomment without an accompanying

compile_units._comment: SQ-family migration TU.  See src/cod/001b0b70.c header.
Per-TU c_flags drop for the one-function TU; drops the global -f=-freorder-blocks so ee-gcc emits the retail bnez+nop branch shape.
VU0 byte-match TU: needs SN compiler for the sq $s0 callee-save prologue (sq prologue).
Same sq family + VU0 idiom as 0027b7.c, needs SN compiler for the sq $s0 callee-save prologue.  See src/cod/0028b0.c header.

sq + lazy-init singleton getter (func_0012CF00).  Needs SN for the sq $s0 callee-save prologue (address-band predictor places 0x0012CF00 deep in band 1 = SN territory).  First crack at the +0x10/nop/-0x20 unusual prologue sub-family (~12 sibling candidates with identical shape).  See src/cod/0012cf.c header.

R-VU0-3: sq+VU0 object constructor (func_002772A8), third validation of the R-VU0 template (after R-VU0 / 0027b7 and R-VU0-2 / 0028b0).  3 sqc2 stores, no interleaved byte-store, plainest R-VU0 sibling shape.  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/002772.c header.

find_template_siblings.py R-VU0 loop continuation: R-VU0-4 sq+VU0 object constructor (func_00278068), fourth validation of the R-VU0 template (5 sqc2 stores).  Plain shape (no byte-store).  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/002780.c header.

find_template_siblings.py R-VU0 loop continuation: R-VU0-5 sq+VU0 object constructor (func_00279CE8), fifth validation; mirror of R-VU0-4 (same 5 sqc2 offsets, different rodata addresses).  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/00279c.c header.

find_template_siblings.py R-VU0 loop continuation: R-VU0-6 sq+VU0 object constructor (func_0027EE68), sixth validation; same 5-sqc2 shape as R-VU0-4 / R-VU0-5.  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/0027ee.c header.

find_template_siblings.py R-VU0 loop continuation: R-VU0-7 sq+VU0 object constructor with 44-iter sqc2 loop (func_0027DD88).  First R-VU0 sibling with a loop body; 6 sqc2 inline + 44 x 6 sqc2 in loop.  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/0027dd.c header.

find_template_siblings.py R-VU0 loop continuation: R-VU0-8 sq+VU0+byte-store object constructor (func_0028E018); R-VU0-2 sub-idiom variant with type-name LUI interleaved into byte-store (forces type_name -> v0 / $2 not v1 / $3).  Pinned to SN for the sq prologue.  See src/cod/0028e0.c header.

TUs containing one or more tail-call (trampoline) functions.  Pinned to cygnus-2.96 because SN ee-gcc 2.95.3-136 does not emit MIPS sibcall sequences.  These tags are redundant with the default but document intent for the bulk migration.
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch A: 4 lazy-init singleton getters at real entry+0x8.  SN-pinned (band 1, sq prologue).
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch B: 15 lazy-init singleton getters (3-arg template), real entry+0x8.  SN-pinned.
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch C: 14 obj-constructor wrappers (store @obj+0xF0 + init call), real entry+0x8.
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch D: small/leaf shapes (id/mask thunks, trivial leaves, non-0xF0 constructor variants), real entry+0x8.  SN-pinned (band 1).
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch E: medium shapes (float-arg thunk, call-chains, flag-test leaf, pointer-arith getter, constructor), real entry+0x8.  SN-pinned (band 1).
merged-orphan boundary-fix batch F: constructor variants with a conditional cleanup block, real entry+0x8.  SN-pinned (band 1).

_carved_funcs_doc: Per-function carves out of the monolithic asm/cod/000000.s
baseline.  Each entry is { name, unit, vaddr, size, tu? }
where `name` is the spim-disasm glabel (matches the function symbol),
`unit` is the source-tree path of the monolithic .s (without suffix),
`vaddr` is the retail virtual address (informational, used for sort
verification), `size` is the function size in bytes (must equal
the `nonmatching <name>, <size>` marker in the monolithic), and
`tu` (optional) names the C TU that owns the carve:
the TU's INCLUDE_ASM macro pulls the carved .s into its own .text,
so the standalone build/asm/nonmatching/<name>.o is NOT built or
linked when `tu` is set.  Without `tu`, the
standalone .o is linked directly — useful for asm-only carves
with no C wrapper yet.
Mechanism: each carve splits the monolithic at the
carve unit boundary (.align 3 ... endlabel <name>); compile.py emits
build/asm/cod/000000.partN.s fragments + build/asm/nonmatching/<name>.s
per carve, then assembles each to its own .o and links via a build-
time copy of the linker script.  Empty list = no-op, byte-identical
build path unchanged.
Example entry shape (do not uncomment without an accompanying
`--reseed-expected <name>` invocation):

carved_funcs._comment: first-TU group — accessor family @ 0x002930A8 – 0x00293C40. 17 carve-eligible funcs (2 Ghidra mis-boundary fragments at 0x002930C8 / 0x00293748 stay in the monolithic).
func_00293138 (fp+bnel, 1500 B) stays in monolithic asm/cod/000000 — PERMANENT; no carve needed.

func_00293760 (jump-table, 96 B) stays in monolithic — carving would leak local .L00293794/.L00293A14/.L002941C0/.L00294308 labels referenced by rodata jump table at 0x44A7B0. PERMANENT under new jump-table class.
func_002937C0 (fp+bnel, 1152 B) stays in monolithic — PERMANENT; no carve needed.
func_00293C40 (fp+bnel, 2116 B) stays in monolithic — PERMANENT; no carve needed.

_rels_doc: Per-REL build descriptors. Each entry produces a single
SNR2-format `.rel` overlay under build/rel/<name>.rel by:
1. Assembling every `asm` part to build/rel/<name>/<asm-rel>.o
2. Full-linking via `mipsel-linux-gnu-ld -EL -T <lcf>
--unresolved-symbols=ignore-all -o build/rel/<name>/<name>.elf`
(full link, not -r; the SNR2 tail
blob already encodes the surviving external reloc table).
3. Extracting .text via
`objcopy -O binary --only-section=.text` to
build/rel/<name>/<name>.text.bin.
4. Concatenating parts in order — each `bin` part is read
verbatim and each `asm` part is replaced by the linked .text
bytes — to produce build/rel/<name>.rel.
5. Asserting sha256(build/rel/<name>.rel) == sha256_expected.
Schema per entry:
name              short identifier (matches splat yaml basename)
lcf               linker script (consumed by step 2)
sha256_expected   on-disc retail sha256 (from disc_extract/rel/manifest.json)
size_expected     on-disc retail byte count (sanity-check vs sha)
build_path        build root for per-REL .o + .elf + .text.bin
parts             ordered list of subsegments. Each is one of:
{kind: 'bin', path: <bin-relative-path>, size: N}
{kind: 'asm', path: <asm-relative-path>, size: N}
The `parts` order MUST match the splat yaml's subsegments order
and total byte count MUST equal size_expected. The verify step
is sibling to scripts/verify_rel_splat_roundtrip.py and exists
to keep the splat round-trip and the build round-
trip wired against the same source-of-truth byte layout.
objdiff units (one per `asm` part) are auto-emitted by `python
compile.py --setup` and live alongside the main-ELF auto units.
expected/build/rel/<name>/... mirrors per-`.o` ground truth with
the same only-if-missing semantics as the main-ELF mirror.
_objdiff_doc: deprecated in the schema; per-unit `target_path`/`base_path` is
used instead).  They live here only as the on-disk roots compile.py
uses when constructing those per-unit paths and when mirroring
`min_version` pins the schema; bumping it should be deliberate.
